theme: cartographers binding an atlas with unequal page sizes
title: Binding the Atlas
seed_problem: bind sorted tiles into a multiway search tree scored by cache-line touches over a weighted lookup + range-scan trace
mutation: composite: fuse an order-constrained (alphabetic) Huffman depth-biasing of hot tiles, a bin-packing of tiles into fixed-width cache lines (fanout vs. line-count via ceil(f/B)), and a range-scan contiguity term (distinct storage pages touched) into one minimized objective, so the clean optimal-BST DP is broken and the three levers must be co-designed.
strategies: trivial: balanced BINARY search tree (one tile per page) = the checker's baseline -> ratio ~0.1
greedy (obvious recipe): uniform B-tree -- pack size-B leaves, balance by count, demand-blind and scan-blind; shortens depth uniformly but never floats a hot tile shallow

insight (strong): weighted-quantile splits float hot tiles to shallow pages (order-constrained Huffman), AND suppress the split-weight of heavily-scanned tiles so a swept band stays packed into few fat pages instead of being shattered -- co-designing depth, fanout and contiguity
beyond strong: jointly optimize page sizing above B (fat root trading extra lines for depth) with per-band packing and exact demand-weighted depth -- intractable, so the ceiling stays open
